##NAMES=Yuzuxiangite
##RRUFFID=R200008
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Sr_3_Fe^3+^(Si_2_O_6_)_2_(OH)·3H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Wessels mine, Kalahari manganese field, Northern Cape Province, South Africa
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Jaco and Tania Janse van Nieuwenhuizen
##DESCRIPTION=Aggregates of light brownish acicular crystals, associated with blue granular meieranite (R140947), colorless hydroxymcglassonite-(K), massive pale green sugilite, prismatic grey aegirine and bladed white pectolite. Cotype sample.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R200008
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Sr_2.94_Pb_0.04_Ca_0.02_)(Fe_0.91_Al_0.09_)[(Si_3.98_Al_0.02_)O_12_] 
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 11.1035 b: 7.8463 c: 7.8222 alpha: 90 beta: 101.406 gamma: 90 volume: 668.02 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
